user_query:
I’m swamped with classes and the news has me on edge. Please help me optimize the on‑page SEO and calm the tone of my local site without changing any body content.

Inputs:
- Read all HTML pages under input/site/ (index.html, study-habits.html, news-thoughts.html), plus input/site/robots.txt and input/site/config.json.

Requirements:
1) For each HTML file under input/site/:
- Extract the current <title>, <meta name="description"> (if any), <meta name="keywords"> (comma-separated), and the first <h1>.
- Rewrite a new title and meta description with a calm, supportive tone (not sensational), keeping clarity and brevity:
  - Title length: 50–60 characters (inclusive).
  - Meta description length: 120–155 characters (inclusive).
  - Must include at least one keyword from the existing <meta name="keywords"> list.
  - Avoid these words (case-insensitive) in both fields: breaking, crisis, panic, urgent, shocking, survive.
  - If a page lacks a meta description, generate one based on the first paragraph content.
- Update the head of the HTML in the output file with:
  - The rewritten <title> and <meta name="description">.
  - Exactly one <meta name="robots" content="index,follow">.
  - A single canonical tag <link rel="canonical" href="[config.site_url + path]"> where path is the file’s URL path (e.g., /index.html, /study-habits.html, /news-thoughts.html). Add if missing or replace if present. Preserve all other markup and body content.

2) Robots and config updates:
- Replace robots.txt settings so search engines can index public pages while avoiding drafts/private areas. In the optimized output, robots.txt should be:
  User-agent: *
  Disallow: /drafts/
  Disallow: /private/
  Allow: /
  Sitemap: [config.site_url]/sitemap.xml
- Modify input/site/config.json in the optimized output to set calmer defaults:
  - site_name: "Calmer Campus Notes"
  - title_suffix: " | Calmer Campus"
  - meta_description_default: "Student wellness, study tips, and balanced notes."
  (Keep other keys unchanged.)

3) Reporting:
- Generate output/report/seo_changes.csv with a header and one row per HTML file containing:
  file_path, original_title, new_title, original_description, new_description, title_length, description_length, keywords (as parsed), keyword_in_title (true/false), keyword_in_description (true/false), banned_terms_found (semicolon-separated if any, else empty), canonical_href (final), changes (short summary of what you modified).

Deliverables:
- Write modified site files to output/optimized/site/, preserving the original filenames and structure (index.html, study-habits.html, news-thoughts.html, robots.txt, config.json).
- Write the report to output/report/seo_changes.csv.

Notes:
- Do not change any body text or links.
- Make the tone steady and reassuring, suitable for stressed students.
- All decisions must be derivable from the provided files (no external lookups). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
